file.content:
}

export interface AnalysisRun {
  id: string
  project_id: string
  triggered_by: string | null
  status: "pending" | "running" | "completed" | "failed"
  global_score: number | null
  total_prompts: number
  citations_found: number
  mentions_found: number
  categories: Record<string, unknown>
  ai_citation: Record<string, unknown> | null
  error_message: string | null
  started_at: string | null
  completed_at: string | null
  created_at: string
}

export interface RunResult {
  id: string
  run_id: string
  project_id: string
  ai_engine: "ChatGPT" | "Perplexity" | "Claude" | "Gemini" | "Copilot"
  prompt: string
  response: string | null
  score: number
  result_type: "citation" | "mention" | "none"
  brand_mentioned: boolean
  url_mentioned: boolean
  competitor_mentioned: boolean
  sentiment: "positive" | "neutral" | "negative" | null
  sentiment_score: number | null
  created_at: string
}

export interface DBRecommendation {
  id: string
  run_id: string
  project_id: string
  priority: "critical" | "high" | "medium" | "low"
  title: string
  description: string
  action: string
  effort: "low" | "medium" | "high"
  impact: "low" | "medium" | "high"
  category: string
  status: "open" | "in_progress" | "done" | "dismissed"
  created_at: string
}

export interface GeneratedContentRow {
  id: string
  project_id: string
  workspace_id: string
  created_by: string | null
  content_type: string
  title: string
  content: string
  meta_description: string | null
  aeo_score: number | null
  aeo_tips: string[] | null
  schema_markup: string | null
  status: "draft" | "review" | "approved" | "published"
  metadata: Record<string, unknown>
  created_at: string
  updated_at: string
}

// ============================================================
// Workspace queries
// ============================================================

export async function getUserWorkspaces(userId: string) {
  const supabase = createClient()
  const { data, error } = await supabase
    .from("workspace_users")
    .select("workspace_id, role, workspaces(*)")
    .eq("user_id", userId)

message.content:
Your task is to create a detailed summary of the conversation so far, paying close attention to the user's explicit requests and your previous actions.
This summary should be thorough in capturing technical details, code patterns, and architectural decisions that would be essential for continuing development work without losing context.

Before providing your final summary, wrap your analysis in <analysis> tags to organize your thoughts and ensure you've covered all necessary points. In your analysis process:

1. Chronologically analyze each message and section of the conversation. For each section thoroughly identify:
   - The user's explicit requests and intents
   - Your approach to addressing the user's requests
   - Key decisions, technical concepts and code patterns
   - Specific details like:
     - file names
     - full code snippets
     - function signatures
     - file edits
  - Errors that you ran into and how you fixed them
  - Pay special attention to specific user feedback that you received, especially if the user told you to do something differently.
2. Double-check for technical accuracy and completeness, addressing each required element thoroughly.

Your summary should include the following sections:

1. Primary Request and Intent: Capture all of the user's explicit requests and intents in detail
2. Key Technical Concepts: List all important technical concepts, technologies, and frameworks discussed.
3. Files and Code Sections: Enumerate specific files and code sections examined, modified, or created. Pay special attention to the most recent messages and include full code snippets where applicable and include a summary of why this file read or edit is important.
4. Errors and fixes: List all errors that you ran into, and how you fixed them. Pay special attention to specific user feedback that you received, especially if the user told you to do something differently.
5. Problem Solving: Document problems solved and any ongoing troubleshooting efforts.
6. All user messages: List ALL user messages that are not tool results. These are critical for understanding the users' feedback and changing intent.
6. Pending Tasks: Outline any pending tasks that you have explicitly been asked to work on.
7. Current Work: Describe in detail precisely what was being worked on immediately before this summary request, paying special attention to the most recent messages from both user and assistant. Include file names and code snippets where applicable.
8. Optional Next Step: List the next step that you will take that is related to the most recent work you were doing. IMPORTANT: ensure that this step is DIRECTLY in line with the user's most recent explicit requests, and the task you were working on immediately before this summary request. If your last task was concluded, then only list next steps if they are explicitly in line with the users request. Do not start on tangential requests or really old requests that were already completed without confirming with the user first.
                       If there is a next step, include direct quotes from the most recent conversation showing exactly what task you were working on and where you left off. This should be verbatim to ensure there's no drift in task interpretation.

Here's an example of how your output should be structured:

<example>
<analysis>
[Your thought process, ensuring all points are covered thoroughly and accurately]
</analysis>

<summary>
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   [Detailed description]

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - [Concept 1]
   - [Concept 2]
   - [...]

3. Files and Code Sections:
   - [File Name 1]
      - [Summary of why this file is important]
      - [Summary of the changes made to this file, if any]
      - [Important Code Snippet]
   - [File Name 2]
      - [Important Code Snippet]
   - [...]

4. Errors and fixes:
    - [Detailed description of error 1]:
      - [How you fixed the error]
      - [User feedback on the error if any]
    - [...]

5. Problem Solving:
   [Description of solved problems and ongoing troubleshooting]

6. All user messages: 
    - [Detailed non tool use user message]
    - [...]

7. Pending Tasks:
   - [Task 1]
   - [Task 2]
   - [...]

8. Current Work:
   [Precise description of current work]

9. Optional Next Step:
   [Optional Next step to take]

</summary>
</example>

Please provide your summary based on the conversation so far, following this structure and ensuring precision and thoroughness in your response. 

There may be additional summarization instructions provided in the included context. If so, remember to follow these instructions when creating the above summary. Examples of instructions include:
<example>
## Compact Instructions
When summarizing the conversation focus on typescript code changes and also remember the mistakes you made and how you fixed them.
</example>

<example>
# Summary instructions
When you are using compact - please focus on test output and code changes. Include file reads verbatim.
</example>


IMPORTANT: Do NOT use any tools. You MUST respond with ONLY the <summary>...</summary> block as your text output.

content.text:
Let me chronologically analyze the conversation: 1. The user provided a massive implementation plan for CitedBy.ai - a complete frontend rebuild of a SaaS AEO application built with Next.js 14 App Router. The plan has 7 phases (0-6) with ~47 files to create/modify. 2. I started by exploring the project structure at `C:\Users\User\Downloads\SaaSV13 - Rick` using an Explore agent (background) and direct file reads. 3. I read all critical existing files to understand the codebase: - types.ts (User, Project, Recommendation, CategoryScore, AnalysisResult, etc.) - package.json (Next.js 14, React 18, Tailwind, Recharts, Supabase, Stripe, etc.) - tailwind.config.ts (dark theme, primary #10b981, animations) - app/layout.tsx (root layout with DM Sans + Instrument Serif fonts) - globals.css (dark theme vars, animations, accessibility) - components/providers.tsx (ThemeProvider + Toaster) - lib/workspace-context.tsx (Supabase-dependent workspace loading) - lib/utils.ts (cn, formatCurrency, formatDate) - app/(app)/app/layout.tsx (auth-gated with Supabase) - app/(app)/app/sidebar.tsx (basic nav items) - app/(app)/app/page.tsx (dashboard with mock data) - i18n/* (context, types, locales, LanguageSelector) - lib/theme-context.tsx - components/ui/* (all 13 UI components) - components/ScoreRing.tsx, CategoryCard.tsx, RecommendationCard.tsx - components/charts/* (visibility-trend, engine-breakdown, competitor) - components/notifications.tsx - All existing app pages (analytics, executive, projects, settings, etc.) - lib/supabase/client.ts and server.ts - lib/db-client.ts and lib/db.ts (database types and queries) - Auth pages (login, register) 4. I created task tracking items: - Task #1: Phase 0 (Foundation) - COMPLETED - Task #2: Phase 1 (Sidebar) - COMPLETED - Task #3: Phase 4A+4B+4C+4D (New pages) - IN PROGRESS - Task #4: Phase 6 (Shared components) - COMPLETED - Task #5: Build verification - PENDING 5. Phase 0 - Created: - lib/mock-data.ts - Central mock data file - lib/mock-auth.tsx - MockAuthProvider + useMockAuth hook - Modified app/(app)/app/layout.tsx - Auth bypass - Modified lib/workspace-context.tsx - Fallback to mock - Modified components/providers.tsx - Added I18nProvider + MockAuthProvider 6. Phase 1 - Sidebar rewrite: - Rewrote app/(app)/app/sidebar.tsx with all nav sections 7. Phase 6 - Shared components: - Created components/page-header.tsx - Created components/kpi-card.tsx - Created components/code-block.tsx - Created components/progress-bar.tsx - Updated components/ui/index.ts 8. Phase 2A - Auth pages: - Modified app/(auth)/layout.tsx - Dark centered with gradient orbs - Modified app/(auth)/login/page.tsx - Mock auth bypass + demo access - Modified app/(auth)/register/page.tsx - Mock auth bypass + name field + terms checkbox 9. Existing pages kept as-is (already had good design): - loading.tsx, error.tsx, not-found.tsx - Landing page (public) - Dashboard, onboarding, projects, analytics, executive, settings pages 10. Launched two background agents for new pages: - Agent a92da84: Analyze page + generative tools (9 files) - Agent a116f7c: Directory + recharge + affiliation + account (5 files) 11. Error encountered: When trying to Write to loading.tsx, error.tsx, not-found.tsx without reading them first, got "File has not been read yet" error. Fixed by reading them first and deciding they were already fine as-is. 12. At the point of summarization, both background agents are still running creating the new pages. Only the directory/page.tsx file had been created so far by the agents. 1. Primary Request and Intent: The user requested a complete frontend rebuild of CitedBy.ai, a SaaS AEO (Answer Engine Optimization) platform built with Next.js 14 App Router. The plan involves ~47 files to create/modify across ~30 routes/pages, organized in 7 phases (0-6). Key goals: - Dark premium design (Linear/Vercel style) with bg-[#0a0a0f], emerald #10b981 primary - Mock data to function WITHOUT Supabase/API keys - Auth bypass when env vars aren't configured - Full sidebar navigation with collapsible sections - New pages: Analyze, Generative Tools (6 sub-tools), Directory, Recharge, Affiliation, Account - Polish existing pages and add shared components - Final build must pass `npm run build` without errors The user also referenced a previous plan transcript at: `C:\Users\User\.claude\projects\C--Users-User\a11f01ea-461c-4cd4-9415-996c5e54cc85.jsonl` 2. Key Technical Concepts: - Next.js 14 App Router with route groups: `(app)`, `(auth)`, `(public)` - Tailwind CSS dark theme with glassmorphism (bg-white/5 backdrop-blur-xl border border-white/10) - Mock auth pattern: `shouldUseMockAuth()` checks if Supabase env vars are placeholders - React Context providers: ThemeProvider, I18nProvider, MockAuthProvider, WorkspaceProvider - Custom i18n system with FR/EN/ES locales using React Context - Supabase SSR auth (server-side) with cookie-based sessions - Recharts for data visualization (AreaChart, BarChart) - Component library: Card, Button, Badge, Input, Modal, Tabs, Select, Textarea, etc. - Fonts: DM Sans (sans) + Instrument Serif (serif italic) - Animations: fade-in-up, scale-in, shimmer (defined in globals.css and tailwind.config.ts) - lucide-react for all icons 3. Files and Code Sections: **CREATED FILES:** - `lib/mock-data.ts` - Central typed mock data for all pages - Exports: MOCK_USER, MOCK_WORKSPACE, MOCK_PROJECTS (3), MOCK_ANALYSIS_RESULT, MOCK_CITATIONS (8), MOCK_RECOMMENDATIONS (6), MOCK_TREND_DATA (8 weeks), MOCK_ENGINE_DATA (5), MOCK_COMPETITOR_DATA (5), MOCK_REPUTATION_REPORT, MOCK_SMC_CONTENTS (4), MOCK_DIRECTORY_ENTRIES (8), MOCK_AFFILIATE_STATS, MOCK_ACTIVITY_FEED (10) - All data is typed against interfaces from types.ts - `lib/mock-auth.tsx` - Mock authentication provider ```tsx "use client" import React, { createContext, useContext, useMemo } from 'react' import { MOCK_USER } from './mock-data' import type { User } from '@/types' interface MockAuthContextValue { user: User isAuthenticated: boolean signOut: () => void } export function MockAuthProvider({ children }: { children: React.ReactNode }) { ... } export function useMockAuth() { return useContext(MockAuthContext) } export function shouldUseMockAuth(): boolean { const url = process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL const key = process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY return !url || url === 'https://placeholder.supabase.co' || !key || key === 'placeholder' } ``` - `components/page-header.tsx` - Reusable page header with title, description, breadcrumbs, actions - `components/kpi-card.tsx` - KPI card with label, value, change indicator, icon - `components/code-block.tsx` - Code display with copy button, language tag, title - `components/progress-bar.tsx` - Animated progress bar with color coding (emerald/amber/orange/red) **MODIFIED FILES:** - `app/(app)/app/layout.tsx` - Auth bypass: uses `shouldUseMockAuth()` to skip Supabase auth, falls back to mock user (email: demo@citedby.ai, id: mock-user-001). Dynamic import of supabase/server only when needed. ```tsx export default async function AppLayout({ children }) { let userEmail = 'demo@citedby.ai'; let userId = 'mock-user-001'; if (!shouldUseMockAuth()) { try { /* Supabase auth */ } catch { /* fallback */ } } return (
{children}
 ); } ``` - `lib/workspace-context.tsx` - Added mock fallback: if `shouldUseMockAuth()` returns true, sets MOCK_WORKSPACE immediately instead of calling Supabase. All catch blocks also fallback to MOCK_WORKSPACE. - `components/providers.tsx` - Added I18nProvider and MockAuthProvider wrapping: ```tsx {children} {mounted && } ``` - `app/(app)/app/sidebar.tsx` - Complete rewrite with: - MAIN_NAV: Dashboard, Analyser, Projets, Analytics, Executif - GENERATIVE_TOOLS (collapsible): Vue d'ensemble, SMC Studio, Content Writer, Reputation, Source of Truth, Schema.org, robots.txt - OTHER_NAV: Annuaire, Credits, Affiliation - SETTINGS_NAV (collapsible): General, Abonnement, API, Integrations - Credits display with progress bar - User section with Avatar, Badge, NotificationBell, LanguageSelector - Account link and logout button - Mobile responsive with hamburger + overlay - `app/(auth)/layout.tsx` - Added gradient orb backgrounds for dark centered design - `app/(auth)/login/page.tsx` - Added mock auth bypass with "Acces demo (sans compte)" button, improved glassmorphism design - `app/(auth)/register/page.tsx` - Added mock auth bypass, name field, terms checkbox, improved design - `components/ui/index.ts` - Added exports for new shared components: ```ts export { PageHeader } from "../page-header" export { KpiCard } from "../kpi-card" export { CodeBlock } from "../code-block" export { ProgressBar } from "../progress-bar" ``` **KEY EXISTING FILES READ (preserved as-is):** - `types.ts` - All TypeScript interfaces (User, Project, Recommendation, CategoryScore, AnalysisResult, SourceOfTruthData, SMCContent, ContentVertical, ContentType, ContentWriterInput, GeneratedContent, ReputationScanInput, AIMention, ReputationReport) - `tailwind.config.ts` - Colors (primary #10b981, background #0a0a0f, etc.), animations, font families - `app/layout.tsx` - Root layout with DM Sans + Instrument Serif fonts, Providers wrapper - `app/globals.css` - CSS variables, animations (fade-in-up, shimmer, scale-in, etc.), accessibility, glass-panel class - `components/ui/*` - 13 production-ready UI components - `components/charts/*` - 3 Recharts components (VisibilityTrendChart, EngineBreakdownChart, CompetitorChart) - `components/ScoreRing.tsx` - Animated SVG score ring (default export) - `components/CategoryCard.tsx` - Expandable category card (default export) - `components/RecommendationCard.tsx` - Recommendation card with solution/generate actions - `components/notifications.tsx` - NotificationBell with mock alerts - `i18n/*` - Full i18n system (context, types, locales FR/EN/ES, LanguageSelector) - `lib/theme-context.tsx` - Dark/light theme toggle - `lib/supabase/client.ts` and `server.ts` - Supabase client creation with placeholder fallbacks - `lib/db-client.ts` - Client-side Supabase queries - `lib/db.ts` - DB types (GeneratedContentRow, DBProject, AnalysisRun, etc.) - All existing app pages (dashboard, projects, analytics, executive, settings, onboarding, etc.) - `app/(public)/page.tsx` - Landing page (already has good design) 4. Errors and Fixes: - **Write without Read error**: When trying to write loading.tsx, error.tsx, and not-found.tsx simultaneously, got "File has not been read yet" error. Fixed by reading all three files first, then deciding they already had good designs and didn't need modification. - No user feedback/corrections were given - the user has not provided any feedback on the work done so far. 5. Problem Solving: - Auth bypass pattern: Solved by creating `shouldUseMockAuth()` that checks if Supabase env vars are missing/placeholder, used consistently across server layout (dynamic import) and client components - Workspace fallback: Modified WorkspaceProvider to immediately return MOCK_WORKSPACE when Supabase is unavailable - Sidebar complexity: Solved with collapsible sections using state + ChevronDown rotation, with initial open state based on current pathname 6. All User Messages: - Message 1: "Implement the following plan: # CitedBy.ai — Refonte Frontend Complète" - Provided the complete implementation plan with 7 phases, dependency graph, file lists, and design direction. Referenced a previous plan transcript file. - Message 2: (System reminder about available skills - no explicit user message, just agent progress notifications) - No other explicit user messages were provided. The user has not given any feedback or corrections. 7. Pending Tasks: - **Task #3 (IN PROGRESS)**: Phase 4A+4B+4C+4D - Create new pages. Two background agents are working: - Agent a92da84: Creating analyze page + generative tools (9 files: analyze/page.tsx, generative-tools/layout.tsx, generative-tools/page.tsx, generative-tools/smc-studio/page.tsx, generative-tools/content-writer/page.tsx, generative-tools/reputation/page.tsx, generative-tools/source-of-truth/page.tsx, generative-tools/schema-org/page.tsx, generative-tools/robots-txt/page.tsx) - Agent a116f7c: Creating directory + recharge + affiliation + account (5 files: directory/page.tsx, directory/[id]/page.tsx, recharge/page.tsx, affiliation/page.tsx, account/page.tsx) - **Task #5 (PENDING)**: Build verification - Run `npm run build` and fix any TypeScript errors - Pages that exist but were NOT explicitly refreshed per the plan: analytics/page.tsx, executive/page.tsx, projects/page.tsx, projects/[id]/* pages, settings/* pages, dashboard page, onboarding page (the plan said "modify" for many of these but they already have good design matching the requirements) - The `app/(public)/page.tsx` landing page was listed for redesign in Phase 2A but already has a complete implementation matching the design spec - `services/gsc_service` import in integrations page may cause build issues (needs checking) 8. Current Work: Immediately before the summary request, I was: - Waiting for two background agents (a92da84 and a116f7c) to finish creating the new pages - Had just read `lib/db.ts` to understand the `GeneratedContentRow` type used in the packs page - Had just read `lib/db-client.ts` to check imports - Had verified that `i18n/LanguageSelector.tsx` was compatible with the sidebar import - Agent a92da84 had used 70916 tokens and was still running (creating analyze + generative tools pages) - Agent a116f7c had used 10523 tokens and was still running (had created directory/page.tsx so far) 9. Optional Next Step: Wait for the two background agents to complete creating all new pages, then: 1. Verify all created files exist with Glob 2. Check for any missing files and create them if needed 3. Run `npm run build` (Task #5) to verify everything compiles 4. Fix any TypeScript errors that emerge from the build This directly continues the implementation plan: "Vérification: 1. `npm run build` — Le build Next.js doit passer sans erreurs"
